name: Privy API
what_it_does: Privy — authentication and embedded wallet infrastructure for web3 apps, enabling users to sign in with social accounts or email and get a non-custodial wallet automatically, with a REST API for user and wallet management.
use_cases: Agents managing user authentication in web3 apps where users need wallets without managing seed phrases
Embedded wallet creation — agents provisioning wallets for users programmatically via Privy's server-side API
Cross-chain identity — agents reading user wallet addresses across multiple chains from a unified Privy user identity
Social login + crypto — agents enabling Google/Apple sign-in that automatically creates a blockchain wallet for users
Server wallets — agents using Privy's server-managed wallets to sign transactions programmatically without private key management
not_for: Non-web3 applications — use Auth0 or Clerk for traditional web auth without blockchain requirements
Self-custody wallet power users — Privy embedded wallets are custodial by design
High-frequency blockchain transactions (>1000 tx/min) — server wallet throughput may be limiting
best_when: Your agent application is web3-native and users need wallets automatically provisioned on sign-up without the friction of external wallet setup.
avoid_when: Your app doesn't need blockchain wallets — traditional auth providers (Clerk, Auth0) are simpler and cheaper.

auth.notes: App ID and App Secret for server-side API. Client-side uses JWT user tokens. Authorization header with Basic auth (App ID:App Secret). User-level tokens for client SDK operations.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 100 monthly active users
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter: $99/month up to 1,000 MAU; Growth: $299/month up to 10,000 MAU; Enterprise: custom
pricing.notes: MAU-based pricing. Server wallet transactions may have additional per-transaction fees at scale. Competitive with Magic and Dynamic for web3 auth pricing.

performance.rate_limits: API: 100 req/min per app; wallet operations: rate-limited per app tier

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Well-documented REST API with TypeScript-first SDK. Error responses include specific codes. Wallet operation errors are clearly categorized. Webhook payload format well-specified.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: User creation by email/social is idempotent — same identity returns existing user. Wallet creation per user is idempotent. Transaction signing is not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Server wallets require separate setup from embedded wallets — different API endpoints and permissions
Privy user IDs are Privy-internal — agents must map to app-level user IDs via linked accounts
Embedded wallets are tied to Privy — migrating off Privy means users lose access to embedded wallets
Social login linking requires user interaction — agents cannot programmatically link accounts without user consent flow
Chain support varies — check current supported chains before building multi-chain agent workflows

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II. Private keys sharded with MPC — Privy cannot unilaterally sign transactions. Non-custodial by design. App Secret for server auth. Hardware security modules for key storage.
